docs.text: Mock BNB Chain Docs This documentation site is used to test the AI docsite update GitHub App. It documents the public behavior of the mock source repository: Source repo: koredeBNB/mock-bsc-app Main documented API: get_validator_status Demo Goal When a pull request is merged in the mock source app, the GitHub App should: receive a GitHub webhook, inspect the merged source diff, identify affected documentation in this MkDocs repo, update the relevant Markdown page, open a pull request for human review.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: This documentation site is used to test the AI docsite update GitHub App. It documents the public behavior of the mock source repository: Source repo: koredeBNB/mock-bsc-app Main documented API: get_validator_status
docs.title: Mock BNB Chain Docs

docs.text: When a pull request is merged in the mock source app, the GitHub App should: receive a GitHub webhook, inspect the merged source diff, identify affected documentation in this MkDocs repo, update the relevant Markdown page, open a pull request for human review.
docs.title: Demo Goal

docs.text: Validators The mock BSC app exposes a small validator status API. get_validator_status Use get_validator_status to read a validator status payload. from mock_bsc_app.validators import get_validator_status status = get_validator_status("validator-1") The function returns a dictionary with these fields: validator_id : the validator identifier passed by the caller. status : the validator status. The current demo value is active . commission_rate : the validator commission rate. Example response: { "validator_id": "validator-1", "status": "active", "commission_rate": 0.05 } Documentation Update Test For the end-to-end demo, change the source API in mock-bsc-app , merge the change into main , and confirm the GitHub App opens a pull request against this documentation repo.
docs.title: Validators
